Our Founder

Jeffrey E. Smith,
Designated Broker

Commercial West Real Estate Advisors, LLC was founded on over 49 years of hands-on experience in Commercial and Industrial Real Estate Development. Jeffrey E. Smith holds a Designated Broker's License in Arizona and has built deep relationships across every major Arizona market.

As owner and executive-in-charge, Jeff has successfully completed nearly $300,000,000 in commercial and industrial development — a testament to his expertise, tireless work ethic, and unwavering commitment to client success.

Commercial West provides commercial brokerage services on behalf of property owners, tenants, and operators throughout Arizona — delivering a distinct competitive advantage for clients buying, selling, leasing, or developing commercial real estate.

Industrial Expertise

Every Type of Industrial Property

From single-tenant distribution warehouses to multi-building manufacturing campuses — we have nearly five decades of experience securing industrial space across Arizona. Whether you need cross-dock logistics in the West Valley, last-mile fulfillment near Sky Harbor, or rail-served manufacturing in Tolleson — we've done it all.

▦

Distribution & Warehouse

Cross-dock, last-mile fulfillment, and high-cube distribution centers across all Arizona logistics corridors.

⚙

Manufacturing

Heavy-power manufacturing facilities with crane bays, drive-in doors, and reinforced flooring.

❄

Cold Storage

Refrigerated and freezer warehouses for food distribution, pharma, and temperature-sensitive logistics.

◆

Flex & Industrial Park

Versatile flex space, industrial condos, and small-bay warehouse parks for growing businesses.

Critical Specifications

What We Evaluate on Every Industrial Search

Spec 01

Clear Height & Loading

Modern logistics need 28'-36' clear heights, cross-dock layouts, and dock-to-bay door ratios that fit your operation.

Spec 02

Power, Utilities & Sprinklers

Heavy power (3-phase, 480V), ESFR sprinkler systems, fiber connectivity, and adequate water/sewer capacity for manufacturing.

Spec 03

Zoning & Truck Access

Industrial zoning compliance, truck court depth, trailer parking, and 24-hour operations permitting where needed.

Common Questions

Arizona Industrial Tenants Frequently Ask

How much does an industrial broker cost?

Nothing — our service is 100% free to tenants and buyers. Industrial real estate landlords pay broker commissions as part of every standard lease or sale transaction. You get expert representation, off-market access, and skilled negotiation at zero out-of-pocket cost.

What clear height do I need for distribution warehouses in Arizona?

Modern Arizona distribution warehouses range from 24' to 40'+ clear height. Last-mile and small-bay tenants can operate at 22'-28'. Regional distribution centers typically require 32'-36' clear. High-cube e-commerce and ASRS-equipped buildings demand 40'+ clear. We'll match your operation to the right inventory.

Where are the best industrial submarkets in Phoenix?

Top Arizona industrial submarkets include the West Valley (Tolleson, Goodyear, Buckeye — Arizona's #1 distribution corridor along I-10), Sky Harbor / South Phoenix (last-mile and airport logistics), East Valley (Mesa, Chandler — manufacturing and semiconductor support), and Loop 303 corridor (modern Class A bulk distribution). Each submarket has different rent ranges and tenant profiles.

How long does it take to find Arizona industrial space?

Most clients sign a lease within 45-120 days. Standard distribution warehouses move faster (45-90 days). Manufacturing facilities with custom power, build-out, or zoning needs require 4-9 months. Build-to-suit projects run 12-18 months from concept to delivery. We recommend starting your search early.

Should I lease or buy my industrial property?

It depends on your capital availability, growth trajectory, and operational stability. Leasing offers flexibility and lower upfront costs. Buying builds equity, locks in your costs long-term, and offers significant tax advantages — especially via SBA 504 financing for owner-users. We provide expert guidance on both, including build-to-suit and sale-leaseback structures.

What is a build-to-suit and how does it work?

A build-to-suit (BTS) is a custom-built industrial facility designed to your exact operational specifications — clear height, dock count, power, layout, and finishes. The developer constructs and owns the building; you sign a long-term lease (typically 10-15 years). It's ideal when no existing facility meets your needs. We've completed BTS projects across all major Arizona industrial markets.

Critical Specifications

What We Evaluate on Every Medical Search

Spec 01

Plumbing & Mechanical

Operatory plumbing, vacuum/compressed air systems, medical gas, dedicated HVAC, and proper ventilation for clinical use.

Spec 02

ADA & Patient Flow

Wheelchair-accessible entries, exam rooms, and restrooms — plus efficient patient-flow layouts that maximize chair-time and revenue.

Spec 03

Parking & Visibility

5/1,000 sf parking ratios for medical use, hospital-campus proximity, and visible signage on major Arizona arterials.

Common Questions

Arizona Healthcare Tenants Frequently Ask

How much does a medical real estate broker cost?

Nothing — our service is 100% free to medical and dental tenants. Healthcare landlords pay broker commissions as part of every standard medical lease. You get expert representation, off-market access, and skilled negotiation at zero out-of-pocket cost to your practice.

What's the difference between medical office and general office space?

Medical office space has specialized infrastructure general office can't match — operatory plumbing, dedicated HVAC zones, ADA-compliant exam rooms, medical gas, vacuum systems, and 5/1,000 parking ratios (vs 4/1,000 for general office). Medical buildings also require landlord experience with healthcare leases — including exclusivity clauses, assignment rights, and HIPAA compliance.

How long does it take to find a medical or dental space?

Pre-plumbed medical/dental suites can be secured in 30-90 days. Custom build-outs (new operatories, surgical suites) typically run 4-8 months from lease signing to opening. Build-to-suit medical office buildings run 12-18 months. Start your search 6+ months before your target opening date to avoid delays.

What's a typical TI allowance for medical and dental space?

Tenant improvement (TI) allowances for Arizona medical/dental space typically run $30-$100+ per square foot depending on building class, specialty, and lease length. Dental and surgical practices get higher allowances ($60-$120+) due to specialized plumbing and equipment infrastructure. We negotiate aggressive TI packages on every lease.

Should I lease or buy my medical/dental practice space?

It depends on your practice maturity, capital, and long-term plans. Leasing offers flexibility for growing or changing practices. Buying — especially via SBA 504 financing — builds equity, locks in occupancy costs, and offers tax benefits ideal for established practices. Many Arizona practices buy their facility within 5-10 years of opening. We provide expert guidance on both.

Why locate near a hospital campus?

Hospital-campus locations drive physician referrals, patient walk-in traffic, and credibility for specialty practices. Banner Health, HonorHealth, Mayo Clinic Scottsdale, Phoenix Children's, and Dignity all anchor major medical districts in Arizona. Trade-offs: higher rents, parking competition, and limited inventory. We help you weigh hospital proximity vs. neighborhood accessibility based on your specialty.

Ready to Find Your Medical or Dental Space?

We'll find the right plumbed, parking-rich, patient-accessible facility — at no cost to you.

Know the Difference

Office Building Classifications Explained

Class A

Premium Buildings

Newest construction, prime locations, top-tier amenities, structured parking, and the highest rents in any market.

Class B

Quality Mid-Tier

Well-maintained older buildings with solid amenities and competitive market rents — often the smartest value play.

Class C

Value Choice

Older buildings with basic amenities and the lowest rents — ideal for cost-conscious businesses prioritizing budget.

Common Questions

Arizona Office Tenants Frequently Ask

How much does it cost to use a tenant rep broker?

Nothing — our service is 100% free to tenants. Commercial real estate landlords pay broker commissions as part of every standard lease transaction. You get expert representation, professional search tools, and skilled negotiation at zero out-of-pocket cost.

What's the difference between Class A, B, and C office space in Arizona?

Class A buildings are premium properties — newest construction, top-tier amenities, prime locations, and the highest rents. Class B buildings are well-maintained older properties with solid amenities at competitive market rates. Class C buildings are older with basic amenities and the lowest rents — best for cost-conscious tenants. We help you choose the right class for your business needs.

How long does it take to find Arizona office space?

Most of our clients sign a lease within 30-90 days from initial contact. The timeline depends on your specific requirements, market availability, and build-out needs. For tenants needing significant tenant improvements, we recommend starting 6+ months before your target move-in date.

What office submarkets are best for tech companies in Arizona?

Top tech-friendly submarkets include Tempe (proximity to ASU + creative loft inventory), Old Town Scottsdale (vibrant urban setting + walkability), Chandler (Intel campus and Price Corridor), and Camelback Corridor (premium Class A office in Phoenix). We'll match you with the submarket that fits your team, talent pipeline, and growth plans.

Should I lease or buy office space in Arizona?

It depends on your business stability, capital availability, and growth trajectory. Leasing offers flexibility and lower upfront costs. Buying builds equity and offers tax benefits but requires capital and locks you in geographically. Commercial West provides expert guidance on both — including build-to-suit and sale-leaseback options.

What's a tenant improvement (TI) allowance and how does it work?

A tenant improvement (TI) allowance is money the landlord provides to customize your office space — paint, flooring, walls, electrical, etc. Typical Arizona office TI allowances range from $15-$60+ per square foot depending on building class, lease length, and market conditions. We negotiate aggressive TI packages as part of every lease.

Ready to Find Your Office Space?

We'll find the right space, at the right price, with lease terms that protect your business — at no cost to you.

Home  /  Resources  /  Industrial Real Estate Arizona
Resource Guide

Industrial Real Estate in Arizona A Complete Guide

Everything you need to know about finding, leasing, and buying industrial property in Arizona's booming commercial market — from one of the state's most experienced industrial brokerages.

● 12 min read ● Updated 2026 ● By Commercial West Real Estate Advisors

On This Page

  • Why Arizona Is Thriving
  • Top Submarkets
  • Leasing Considerations
  • FAQ

Arizona has emerged as one of the most desirable industrial real estate markets in the United States. With its strategic location, business-friendly regulatory environment, and a rapidly growing population base providing a strong labor pool, Arizona industrial properties are in high demand from businesses of every size and sector.

Top 5
U.S. Industrial Market
I-10/17/40
Major Freight Corridors
UP & BNSF
Class I Rail Service

Why Arizona's Industrial Market Is Thriving

The greater Phoenix area is now one of the top five industrial real estate markets nationally. Several factors drive this remarkable growth:

  • Prime Location: Arizona sits at the intersection of major rail, highway, and air freight corridors, making it ideal for distribution and logistics operations.
  • Population Growth: Phoenix metro is one of the fastest-growing regions in the U.S., fueling demand for consumer goods distribution and last-mile delivery facilities.
  • Business Climate: Low corporate taxes, streamlined permitting, and pro-business government policies attract manufacturers and distributors from California and other high-cost states.
  • Infrastructure: Major interstates (I-10, I-17, I-40), Union Pacific and BNSF rail lines, and Phoenix Sky Harbor Airport create unmatched freight connectivity.
  • Land Availability: Unlike coastal markets, Arizona still has developable industrial land at competitive prices compared to Southern California or Seattle.
Arizona's combination of freight access, population growth, and business-friendly policy has made Phoenix one of the few U.S. industrial markets where supply and demand are growing in lockstep. — Commercial West Market Analysis

Arizona's Top Industrial Submarkets

Southwest Phoenix / Laveen

One of the fastest-growing industrial corridors. Massive logistics facilities with I-10 and Loop 202 access.

Goodyear / Buckeye

Fastest-growing large-format industrial area. Major e-commerce fulfillment centers and rail access.

Chandler / Gilbert

Advanced manufacturing, semiconductor, and high-tech industrial. Intel drives significant development.

Deer Valley / North Phoenix

Established industrial parks near Deer Valley Airport — ideal for aerospace and precision manufacturing.

Key Considerations When Leasing Industrial Space

  • Clear Height: Modern distribution demands 32'–40' clear height. Know your racking requirements before your search.
  • Power Supply: Heavy manufacturing requires 480V 3-phase power. Confirm transformer capacity before committing to any space.
  • Dock Doors vs. Grade Level: Distribution needs dock-high loading; service businesses often prefer grade-level drive-in doors.
  • Truck Court Depth: Adequate truck court (130'+ for semi-trailer access) is critical for logistics and distribution facilities.
  • Zoning: Industrial zoning designations vary by municipality and determine allowed uses, including outdoor storage and fleet parking.
  • HVAC: Arizona's extreme summer heat makes warehouse HVAC critical for both product protection and employee retention.

Frequently Asked Questions

What are current industrial lease rates in Arizona?

Industrial lease rates in Phoenix generally range from $0.75 to $1.50+ per sq ft/month NNN for bulk distribution, and $1.00 to $2.00+ for smaller flex product. Contact us for a current market analysis specific to your size requirement and location.

How long does it take to find industrial space in Arizona?

Simple searches for existing space can move to lease execution in 30–60 days. More complex searches or build-to-suit projects can take 3–12 months. Starting early gives you the greatest negotiating leverage.

Do I need a broker to lease industrial space in Arizona?

Working with an experienced industrial broker is strongly recommended. Our tenant representation costs you nothing — our commission is typically paid by the landlord — and provides expert negotiation, market knowledge, and access to off-market opportunities unavailable to the public.

What is the difference between industrial and flex space?

Traditional industrial space is primarily warehouse/manufacturing with limited office. Flex space blends office and warehouse in more balanced proportions (typically 20–50% office) — popular with service businesses and distributors who need showroom space or receive clients on-site.

Resource Guide

Warehouse Space for Lease & Sale in Phoenix, Arizona

Your definitive guide to finding, evaluating, and securing the right warehouse space in the greater Phoenix metro area — from cold storage to bulk distribution.

● 14 min read ● Updated 2026 ● By Commercial West Real Estate Advisors

On This Page

  • Types of Warehouse Space
  • How to Evaluate
  • Lease vs. Purchase
  • FAQ

Phoenix has become one of America's premier warehouse and distribution markets. Whether you need a small 2,000 sq ft flex warehouse or a 500,000 sq ft regional distribution center, the greater Phoenix metro offers a diverse inventory of modern, well-located industrial facilities to support your business operations.

2,000+
Square Feet (entry)
500K+
Sq Ft Distribution
$0.65–$1.75
PSF/Month NNN Range

Types of Warehouse Space Available in Phoenix

Bulk Distribution Centers (100,000+ sq ft)

Large-format distribution centers concentrated in the West Valley (Goodyear, Buckeye, Tolleson) and Southwest Phoenix. These facilities feature 32–40 ft clear heights, cross-dock loading configurations, ample trailer storage, and immediate freeway access via I-10 and Loop 202.

Mid-Size Warehouses (10,000 – 100,000 sq ft)

Regional distribution, manufacturing, and specialty industrial users are served by this broad mid-market segment. Available throughout all Phoenix submarkets, with strong concentrations in Chandler, Mesa, and Deer Valley/North Phoenix.

Small Bay Industrial / Flex (1,500 – 10,000 sq ft)

Perfect for small businesses, service companies, contractors, and light manufacturers. Multi-tenant business parks offer affordable entry-level industrial space with grade-level access and modest office components — often under $2,000 per month all-in for smaller spaces.

Cold Storage & Refrigerated Warehouses

Specialized facilities for food distribution, pharmaceutical storage, floral, and other temperature-sensitive goods. A growing asset class in the Phoenix market, with significant tenant improvement costs for new cold-chain installations.

The biggest mistake we see Phoenix tenants make is matching warehouse specs to today's operation — instead of where their business will be in 24 months. Plan for growth. — Jeffrey E. Smith, Designated Broker

How to Evaluate a Phoenix Warehouse

  • Clear Height: Older Phoenix warehouses often have 20–24 ft clear; modern buildings offer 32–40 ft. Know your racking requirements before your search.
  • Dock Doors: How many dock-high doors? What is the spacing? Are levelers, seals, and bumpers included or additional cost?
  • Power: What is the incoming electrical service? Many manufacturing and e-commerce operations require 200A or more.
  • Office Ratio: What percentage is air-conditioned office vs. warehouse? Can the ratio be modified under the lease?
  • Sprinkler System: ESFR sprinklers are required for high-rack storage over certain heights — confirm before selecting a space.
  • Truck Court Depth: Adequate truck court and trailer staging are critically important but frequently overlooked during warehouse selection.
  • Zoning & Permitted Uses: Confirm your specific operation is a permitted use — outdoor storage, vehicle fleets, and chemical storage are commonly restricted by local zoning codes.

Warehouse Lease vs. Purchase in Phoenix

Leasing offers flexibility, preserves capital, and typically includes landlord responsibility for structural repairs. Ideal for businesses with evolving space needs or those who want to deploy capital into their core operations rather than real estate ownership.

Purchasing builds equity, provides full operational control, and can offer meaningful tax advantages through depreciation. Owner-user industrial properties in Phoenix have historically appreciated well, making purchase an attractive long-term strategy for established businesses with stable space needs.

Commercial West can guide you through both options and model the financial implications of each for your specific situation — at no cost.

Frequently Asked Questions

What are warehouse lease rates in Phoenix?

Phoenix warehouse lease rates range from approximately $0.65 to $1.75+ per sq ft/month NNN, depending on building size, age, submarket, and specifications. Smaller flex spaces tend to lease at higher per-sq-ft rates than large-format bulk distribution facilities.

How much warehouse space does my business need?

Calculate your current storage cube requirements, add 20–30% for aisles and staging, then add your office and ancillary needs. We recommend starting with a real-world operational layout so you don't over-lease or under-lease and face expansion costs within a year or two.

Is it better to lease or buy a warehouse in Phoenix?

For most early-stage or growing businesses, leasing offers the flexibility needed as operations scale. For established businesses with stable space needs and strong balance sheets, purchasing can be financially advantageous. We can model both options with current market data at no cost to you.

How does Commercial West help me find warehouse space?

We begin with a thorough needs analysis, search professional databases not available to the public, prepare a custom availability report, arrange tours, and negotiate terms on your behalf — as tenant representatives, our fee is typically paid by the landlord at no cost to you.

Scottsdale, Arizona is home to some of the most desirable commercial real estate in the American Southwest. From the prestigious Scottsdale Airpark industrial complex to the high-image Class A office towers along the Loop 101 corridor, Scottsdale offers a premium business environment for companies across all industries.

The Scottsdale Commercial Real Estate Market

Scottsdale's commercial market is characterized by quality, prestige, and resilience. The city's strong economy, affluent population, world-class tourism industry, and business-friendly environment make it one of the most sought-after commercial addresses in the entire American Southwest.

Scottsdale Airpark: A World-Class Business Community

The Scottsdale Airpark deserves special mention as one of the most unique commercial and industrial environments in Arizona. Anchored by Scottsdale Airport (SDL) — the world's busiest single-runway general aviation airport — the Airpark hosts over 2,500 businesses:

  • Aviation MRO: Maintenance, repair, and overhaul facilities
  • Corporate Aviation: Hangars and fixed-base operators (FBOs)
  • Advanced Manufacturing: High-tech industrial and precision manufacturing
  • Corporate HQs: Headquarters and regional offices for major brands
  • Showroom Space: High-image retail and dealership facilities
  • Healthcare: Medical and dental office facilities

Commercial West, headquartered at 15657 N. Hayden Road in Scottsdale, has deep roots in the Airpark market and maintains strong relationships with major Airpark landlords — giving our clients access to opportunities before they hit the open market.

The Scottsdale Airpark is the only place in Arizona where a corporate jet, a high-tech manufacturer, a dental practice, and a luxury auto showroom can all be neighbors — and all benefit from it. — Commercial West Market Insights

Medical & Dental Real Estate in Scottsdale

Scottsdale's affluent, established demographic makes it one of Arizona's strongest markets for medical and dental practices. Demand for healthcare services in North Scottsdale has driven significant medical office development along Scottsdale Road, Frank Lloyd Wright Blvd, and Pima Road.

  • Proximity to major hospital systems (HonorHealth, Banner, Mayo Clinic)
  • Strong visibility and signage opportunities for patient-facing practices
  • High parking ratios that accommodate patient flow and staff needs
  • Building infrastructure compatible with specialty medical equipment

The Commercial West Scottsdale Advantage

Commercial West Real Estate Advisors is headquartered in Scottsdale. This means genuine local market knowledge that goes far beyond what any national brokerage firm can offer. We know which buildings are planning renovations, which landlords are motivated, and which spaces are coming available before they're marketed.

Our founder Jeff Smith has been active in the Scottsdale commercial market for decades, building relationships that directly benefit our clients in the form of better spaces, better terms, and faster transactions.

Frequently Asked Questions

What types of commercial property are available in Scottsdale?

Scottsdale offers an exceptionally diverse commercial inventory: Class A office (Loop 101 corridor), industrial and Airpark space (Scottsdale Airpark), medical office (North Scottsdale corridors), retail (Old Town, Scottsdale Quarter, Kierland), flex/industrial, and specialty hospitality properties. Almost every commercial property type is well-represented.

How do Scottsdale lease rates compare to Phoenix?

Scottsdale typically commands a premium of 10–25% over comparable Phoenix properties due to its prestige, demographics, and quality of built environment. Class A office can range from $30–$55+ per sq ft annually. Airpark industrial also commands a meaningful premium over comparable Phoenix industrial product.

Is the Scottsdale Airpark a good location for my business?

The Scottsdale Airpark is ideal for businesses that benefit from aviation access, high-image industrial facilities, proximity to North Scottsdale's executive workforce, or the cross-pollination of 2,500+ co-located Airpark businesses. It is one of Arizona's most prestigious business addresses — and Commercial West is ideally positioned to help you secure space there.

Does Commercial West offer tenant representation in Scottsdale?

Yes — tenant representation is one of our core services in Scottsdale and throughout Arizona. We represent you exclusively, advocate for your interests, and negotiate the best possible lease or purchase terms. Our fee is typically paid by the landlord, making tenant representation a no-cost service for qualifying clients.
